It’s going to be another total loss for this Wildberries distribution center near Moscow in Podolsk following a Ukrainian drone attack. For the second time in a week Ukrainian drones have broken through Russian air defenses around the capital to strike at a warehouse for Russia’s biggest online retailer. The goals of these attacks are to disrupt Russian logistics, but also to further impact the Russian economy whilst also bringing the war home to Muscovites. Similar strikes are expected to continue…. There are a great deal of distribution centers in range and they are much less well-defended than those in Moscow.

RT by @no_itsmyturn: The Darkhovin nuclear reactor construction site reportedly attacked: Not such a squeaky clean reactor project From an analysis we did in 2024: “The Darkhovin nuclear power reactor, aka IR-360 or Karun nuclear power reactor, has crystalized as another safeguards anomaly and standoff with the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA). This reactor and planned ones pose export control challenges, both as to Iran’s illicit acquisition of equipment and technology abroad and its potential spread of these goods to other countries.” We are seeking satellite imagery to see if any damage and, if so, its extent. https://isis-online.org/isis-reports/darkhovin-nuclear-power-reactor-another-blemish-on-irans-safeguards-complia

RT by @no_itsmyturn: Since we're all talking about transparency and FARA, here's an example of how Qatari agents operate. GRV Strategies LLC is paid $88K a month by Qatar's International Media Office for PR and consulting. It also recieved an unexplained extra $580K payment in December. GRV told the DOJ that its entire disclosed "political activity" for Qatar between October 1, 2025 and March 31, 2026 was one text to Speaker Mike Johnson's office and three media pitches. That's $1,108,000 for a text message and three emails. Is this all the firm did for Qatar during that period? Color me skeptical. The American public deserves transparency.

Yes this was obvious but from the Kurdish perspective, across the political spectrum the Israelis cannot be trusted in such an endeavor, nor can the Americans. The Israelis were more than willing to give rhetorical support for Kurdish independence in the past but did not lift a finger to do anything else. Trump 1 unilaterally withdrew from parts of Syria, leading to a Turkish invasion. Trump 2 was really not that interested in the integration process between the SDF and STG, where American pressure may (highly arguable) have prevented its breakdown. Trump had in 2025 already abruptly halted the war against Iran, much to Israel’s displeasure. So what’s the appeal? Kurdish revolutionaries launch an uprising and seize some cities? Then get crushed over the course of months to a year after air support stops and the Iranian regime buries the revolt under artillery and tank fire? Not to mention the widespread crackdowns that would follow. The Americans are already very quiet and limiting regarding defenses, let alone a military response, to hundreds of drone and missile attacks across the border into Kurdish areas. No political guarantees, no security guarantees. At least publicly, which would be necessary. There’s zero appeal.

RT by @lookner: The IAEA is looking into reports of an overnight attack on the construction site of a planned nuclear power plant in Darkhovin, Iran. The facility is in the very early stages of construction and contained no nuclear material when last visited by the IAEA. While the reported attack is not believed to pose any radiological risk, DG @rafaelmgrossi reiterates call for military restraint in the vicinity of all nuclear-related sites. The IAEA has contacted Iran about the public reports and will provide further information as it becomes available.

RT by @IntelDoge: Worldwide Caution: Due to heightened tensions in the Middle East, the security environment remains complex with the potential for unforeseen escalation. We remind Americans in the region of the continued need for caution and encourage them to monitor the news for breaking developments.  The Department of State advises Americans worldwide, and especially in the Middle East, to exercise increased caution. Americans abroad should follow the guidance in security alerts issued by the nearest U.S. embassy or consulate. Flight cancellations and periodic airspace closures may cause travel disruptions. U.S. diplomatic facilities, including outside the Middle East, have been targeted. Groups supportive of Iran may target other U.S. interests overseas or locations associated with the United States and/or Americans throughout the world. Please read carefully our Travel Advisory, country information, and any recent security alerts when planning travel at http://travel.state.gov/destination. Full alert: https://travel.state.gov/en/international-travel/travel-advisories/global-events/worldwide-caution.html.
